title: FULL OUTER JOIN
description: Give me absolutely everything you have.

slides.title: The Ultimate Glue 🌍
slides.content:
# Keep it all!

`INNER JOIN` is strictly exclusive.
`LEFT JOIN` only forgives the left side.
`RIGHT JOIN` only forgives the right side.

But what if you want **every single user**, and **every single order**, regardless of if they match or not?

slides.title: The FULL OUTER JOIN 👐
slides.content:
## Nobody left behind

The **`FULL OUTER JOIN`** guarantees survival for everything. 

If Bob has no orders? He's in. If Order #50 has no matching user? It's in. 

`SELECT * FROM users FULL OUTER JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id;`

The database will generously pad `NULL`s on whichever side is missing data.

quizData.question: If you perform a `FULL OUTER JOIN`, which records are systematically discarded?

options.text: Absolutely none. Every record from both tables survives.

quizData.explanation: FULL OUTER JOIN is the most inclusive join possible, uniting both tables entirely and padding holes with NULLs.

quizData.question: If an order has no user, where are the NULLs placed in the resulting grid?

options.text: In the user's columns (e.g., first_name, email)

quizData.explanation: Because the order data inherently exists, it displays. Because the user data is missing, those specific columns are populated with NULLs.
